subject: Rachel Miller PwC call request
draftApproach: offered quick call this week, available most afternoons
kurtisApproach: gave specific time windows: today 2-5pm PST, tomorrow 11am-2pm PST
lesson: Be more specific with availability - offer exact time blocks instead of vague most afternoons

subject: Partner Summit Social Wall - Lynley onsite support
draftApproach: said yes to all 4 days onsite support, listed basic tech needs
kurtisApproach: clarified $650 is for tech coming BACK onsite for issues, not staying all 4 days. Offered $200/hr onsite tech as separate line item if needed
lesson: Understand pricing model before responding — $650 onsite support ≠ all-day staffing. Ask about pricing structure rather than assuming

subject: Live oak school party - Morgan Metcalf
draftApproach: drafted standard quote reply to Morgan directly
kurtisApproach: forwarded to Coreen to handle — delegated to team member
lesson: Some leads get delegated to team. Watch for patterns of which leads Kurtis handles vs delegates

subject: AI Activation April 22 - Emiliano device question
draftApproach: Offered to source Google tablets as alternative to iPads, promised follow-up quote for additional cost
kurtisApproach: Just replied 'Thanks!' — acknowledged Emiliano's acceptance of the group selection arrangement without addressing the Google tablet ask directly
lesson: Don't overcommit to sourcing alternative hardware. Kurtis kept it simple. If the client says they're comfortable with the arrangement, a simple acknowledgment is enough — don't volunteer to change the setup unless specifically pressed

draft_approach: Offered AI Photo Booth and Glam Booth as alternatives to aura booth
kurtis_approach: Flat no - unfortunately we dont have that experience
lesson: When we dont have a specific product, Kurtis prefers honest no over offering alternatives that arent truly equivalent. Dont oversell substitutes.

subject: Re: Signed Contract (Abe Lee)
my_draft: Longer response addressing both points with extra detail about gallery sharing process
kurtis_sent: Short 2-sentence reply: confirmed no signs, confirmed digital gallery included at no cost
lesson: Keep replies SHORT. Kurtis uses 1-2 sentences max for simple confirmations. Don't over-explain.

subject: Photo Booth- LA Event
myDraft: Sent both staffed + self-service quotes immediately
kurtisActual: Asked which option first (staffed or self-service?) before quoting
lesson: For returning clients asking about availability, ask clarifying questions first rather than sending all options. More conversational.

subject: Lauren-Ashley Soriano Corporate Brand Activation
my_draft: Acknowledged 2 cutters + PDF invoice + graphic for cards — straightforward confirmation
kurtis_sent: Asked her to confirm full order via quote link + added urgency about custom case sticker design deadline (tomorrow morning) + asked for logo/design ASAP
delta: Kurtis turned the reply into a confirmation step + added time pressure on design deadline. I was too agreeable — should have asked her to confirm the full order and flagged the sticker design urgency.
lesson: When a booking is being finalized, push for order confirmation and flag production deadlines rather than just acknowledging requests.

pattern: Kurtis uses standardized reply template: 'Hey [Name], Thanks for reaching out about your upcoming event! I've put together a quote for you here: KANDE QUOTE. It includes the experiences you were interested in with half/full/extended day pricing. The quote is dynamic and will adjust pricing based on the items you add to it. Let me know if you have any questions?' — minimal customization per lead. My drafts were over-customized (mentioning specific booth types, guest counts, travel fees, asking discovery questions). Kurtis keeps it SHORT and UNIFORM.
examples: Darlene Tacorda: My draft mentioned 'open air photo booth' + keychain discussion. Kurtis just said 'experiences you were interested in' + 'added keychain favors'.
Jessica Cobb: My draft asked about venue and album theme customization. Kurtis just sent standard quote template.
Trom Procter: My draft suggested chatting about setup for 3000+ guests. Kurtis just asked about dates/times — one practical question.
lesson: Match Kurtis's actual style: SHORT standard template + at most ONE practical question. Don't over-personalize or add discovery questions he doesn't ask.

my_draft: Included travel fee heads-up for Walnut Creek (NorCal)
kurtis_sent: Standard template with 'full day pricing'. No travel fee mention.
lesson: Kurtis doesn't proactively mention travel fees in the initial quote email. He lets the quote itself handle pricing details. Don't add caveats about travel/distance in drafts.

subject: Re: SF Whitelabel needed 3/10 & 3/24
my_draft: Declined 3/10 DSLR roaming — said we dont have that setup. Offered to quote 3/24 stationary iPad booth only, asked for venue and guest count.
kurtis_sent: Accepted BOTH dates. Quoted $1800 for 3/24 with payment link, asked for venue/event details. For 3/10, confirmed can do roaming photographer with DSLR + snappic, asked for EOD confirmation.
lesson: Dont decline capabilities without checking. Kurtis confirmed they CAN do DSLR roaming with snappic. Also, quote price immediately with payment link instead of asking discovery questions. Be decisive and close.

subject: 6/25/26 FIGR Graduation
my_draft: Said would check on message, confirm 1hr credit, get a quote together shortly. Wordy, non-specific.
kurtis_sent: Sent quote link immediately with complimentary 1hr already included. Updated template for 2026 and attached it. Proactively addressed backdrop question — will have team look for it, offered alternatives if not found. Concise and action-oriented.
lesson: For returning clients, send the quote IMMEDIATELY with everything already set up. Dont say youll get a quote together shortly — just do it. Address every question in one reply with concrete actions.

subject: Re: Kande Photo Booths - checking in | Amber Mullins
my_draft: Sounds great, thanks for the update Amber! Looking forward to hearing back.
kurtis_sent: (Mary replied) Sure, sounds good. Please feel free to reach out if you have any questions.
lesson: Mary's reply was nearly identical to my draft. Good match for this type of waiting-on-client response.

subject: Inquiry – Adobe Summit Booth Activation
my_draft: Confirmed both options (trading cards + multi-camera), sent standard quote template
kurtis_sent: Called her first ('great chatting with you earlier'), then sent detailed quote pre-configured for 3 full days (up to 8hrs/day) with extended hour add-ons. More detailed than standard template because high-value corporate agency.
lesson: For major corporate/agency inquiries, Kurtis calls first then follows up with a detailed, pre-configured quote. Pre-set the quote duration to match their event. Standard template OK but add specifics about the pricing structure when it's multi-day.

subject: New Lead for Jenny Ha
my_draft: Added 'The custom trading cards are a ton of fun for corporate parties' — over-personalized per 3/7 feedback
kurtis_sent: Standard template exactly: 'experiences you were interested in with half day pricing.' No commentary about trading cards being fun.
lesson: Confirms standard template lesson. Don't add color commentary about specific experiences. Keep it uniform.

subject: RE: 6/25/26 FIGR Graduation
my_draft: Said we will update colors and get backdrop remade, send proof when ready
kurtis_sent: Sent updated mockup image directly in the reply. Quick and visual — showed the work rather than promising it.
lesson: For design/visual requests from returning clients, Kurtis creates and sends the mockup immediately rather than saying 'I'll send you a proof when ready.' Show, don't tell.

subject: Re: Jenny Ha Holiday Party Trading Cards (follow-up questions)
my_draft: Said multiple people can fit, unlimited sessions. Vague on specifics.
kurtis_sent: Linked to sample setup page (kandephotobooths.com/custom-trading-cards/), said 10 guests could fit if squished in layers, each card takes 2-3 min, suggested additional cutting stations for throughput, confirmed unlimited within service time. Very specific and practical.
lesson: Link to product pages for visual reference instead of promising to send photos. Give specific numbers (2-3 min per card, throughput considerations). Offer upsell naturally (additional cutting stations).

subject: RE: FIGR Graduation (Ali color request)
my_draft: Said we'll update colors and get backdrop remade, will send proof when ready.
kurtis_sent: Sent updated mockup immediately ('see here for an updated mockup'), mentioned needing to invoice for custom backdrop, showed design options to choose from.
lesson: Send the actual mockup/proof immediately rather than saying you'll send it later. Mention cost implications upfront (invoicing for custom backdrop). Give options to choose from.

subject: Re: Jenny Ha Holiday Party Trading Cards (follow-up questions)
my_draft: Said multiple people can fit, unlimited sessions and prints, offered to send photos. Vague on specifics.
kurtis_sent: Gave specific link to sample setup (kandephotobooths.com/custom-trading-cards/), said 10 can fit if they stand in layers and squish, each card takes 2-3 min, suggested additional cutting stations for throughput. Followed up again re: digital copies — explained no onsite sharing due to cutting template, but post-event gallery available.
lesson: Be specific with product details: link to sample pages, give actual capacity numbers and production times, suggest upsells (additional cutting stations). Kurtis knows the product specs — I should too. Also note: trading cards dont include onsite digital sharing because of cutting templates.

subject: Looking for photo booth options for party
kurtisEdits: Kurtis simplified significantly - removed guest count recommendations, removed multiple booth suggestion, shorter and more concise, just quote link + let me know questions
lesson: Keep initial quote emails SHORT - just quote link + questions. Don't over-recommend or suggest multiple booths in first contact.

subject: Events at Stag Leap Wine Cellars
kurtisEdits: Kurtis simplified - removed wine/napa commentary, removed outdoor logistics mention, mentioned Magazine cover booth specifically, offered contract for all 3 events
lesson: Don't commentary on client's event theme. Suggest specific product match. Offer to move forward on all events at once.

subject: Vogue Photo Booth Warriors
kurtisEdits: Kurtis went personal - tried calling first, asked for callback. Very short email. No quote link in initial contact.
lesson: For HIGH VALUE corporate leads, Kurtis prefers phone-first approach - call then short email asking for callback. Don't send detailed quote email as first touch.

subject: Trade show activation Deloitte
kurtisEdits: Kurtis replied similarly to draft but mentioned working with Deloitte before and included multi-day discount. Lead declined (over budget), Kurtis followed up gracefully.
lesson: Mention past work with client company if applicable. Include multi-day discounts proactively.

subject: Fundbox Brand Event
draft_diff: Draft said 'with pricing'; Kurtis added 'about your upcoming event' and specified 'half/full/extended day pricing'. Minor wording enhancement.

subject: Madeline Roy Multiple Events
draft_diff: Draft suggested scheduling a call to discuss details. Kurtis instead sent the quote directly with KANDE QUOTE link and said 'once you figure out the dates/locations and experiences, I can create separate invoices for each event.' Lesson: For multi-event leads, still send the quote upfront rather than asking for a call. Let them browse options first.

subject: Flavia Duarte follow-up questions
draft_diff: Draft gave 10x10ft + 10x14ft total space. Kurtis said 16x8ft, condensable to 10x8ft. Different dimensions - Kurtis knows actual setup specs better. Also Kurtis used inline Q&A format (pasting question then answering below) rather than paragraph format.

subject: Andrea Perez MPA Open House
draft_snippet: A corporate open house sounds like a great event...includes pricing for our photo booth experience
actual_snippet: Thanks for reaching out about your upcoming event...includes the experiences you were interested in with half/full/extended day pricing
diff_notes: Kurtis replaced generic 'photo booth experience for your event' with specific 'experiences you were interested in with half/full/extended day pricing'. Also changed 'Thanks for reaching out!' to 'Thanks for reaching out about your upcoming event!'. Minor style adjustments.
